english: to decline, to sink, to get weak, frail, esp. with old age Mil., Pth.; in W. used in a more general sense: *gud soṅ* (gud song) 1. he has grown thin. 2. he is impoverished, much reduced, in declining circumstances; dar-rgúd (dar rgud) the rise and fall in the world.

the vine; grape; rgun-dkár (rgun dkar) white grapes, rgun-nág (rgun nag) black, or purple grapes W.; rgun-rgód (rgun rgod) W. raisins; rgun-_obrum (rgun 'brum) grapes; raisins; rgun(-_obrum)-śiṅ (rgun 'brum shing) vine; rgun(-_obrum)-tsás (rgun 'brum tsas) vineyard; rgun-cáṅ (rgun chang) Mil. wine, resp. rgun-skyéms (rgun skyems) Cs.
1. laughing, laughter S.g. — 2. bird of prey. — 3. wild. — 4. prudent (v. the following word).

I vb. 1. to laugh, aloud Mil.; (Bal. *rgód-ċa* (rgod cha)) cf. gád-mo (gad mo), dgód-pa (dgod pa), bgád-pa (bgad pa). — 2. to grow weak, languid, or indolent, syn. to ɣyéṅ-ba (g.yeng ba), often joined with byiṅ (bying), for emphasis; rgód-bag-càn (rgod bag can) weak, languid, indolent Stg. II. adj. 1. wild, ra-rgód (ra rgod) wild goat, phag-rgód (phag rgod) wild boar, ɣyag-rgód (g.yag rgod) wild yak or ox; rgod-ɣyag-rwá (rgod g.yag rwa) horn of the wild yak S.g.; bya-rgód (bya rgod) vulture, bird of prey = bya-rgyál (bya rgyal); rgód-po (rgod po), and rgod (rgod) id.; rgód-kyi rtse-rgyál (rgod kyi rtse rgyal) an eagle's feather, stuck as ornament on the hat Pth.; mi-rgód (mi rgod) a wild or savage man; a robber, ruffian; mi-rgód byéd-pa (mi rgod byed pa) to rob (usually named together with murdering and lying) Dzl., but as the Tibetan always attaches to this word mi-rgód (mi rgod) the notion of some gigantic hairy fiend, it cannot in every instance be applied to beings really existing. — Fig. furious, angry (seldom); dbugs-rgód (dbugs rgod) Med.? — 2. prudent, able C., Zam.
rta rgód-ma (rta rgod ma) (Bal. *gún-ma* (gun ma)) a mare; rgod-skám-ma (rgod skam ma) a bar-ren mare Sch.; rgod-brún (rgod brun) dung of a mare Med.
1. v. rgál-ba (rgal ba). 2. v. rgól-ba (rgol ba).

pf. and fut. brgol (brgol), to dispute, combat, fight, mi-la (mi la) with or against a man; ṗá-rol-poi dmág-la rgól-du _ogró-ba (pha rol po'i dmag la rgol du 'gro ba) they are about to fight against the hostile army Dzl.; ɣźán-gyis rgol ma nús-so (gzhan gyis rgol ma nus so) nobody could fight them, could make head against them Glr.; to offer resistance, to make opposition, sus kyaṅ rgól-ba _odzúgs-pai mi ma byuṅ (sus kyang rgol ba 'dzugs pa'i mi ma byung) nobody arose to offer resistance Pth. (evidently incorrect; it should be either; sus kyaṅ rgól-ba _odzúgs-pa (sus kyang rgol ba 'dzugs pa) [inf.] ma byuṅ (ma byung), or: rgól-ba _odzúgs-pai (rgol ba 'dzugs pa'i) [partic.] mi su yaṅ ma byuṅ (mi su yang ma byung)); sometimes as much as to accuse, to charge, khyód-kyis ṅai bu bsád-do źes brgál-te (khyod kyis nga'i bu bsad do zhes brgal te) 'you have killed my son', thus accusing me Dzl.; tsur ɣnyis rgol (tsur gnyis rgol) he quarrels at a double rate Mil.; mi-la rgól-ba źú-ba (mi la rgol ba zhu ba) to find fault with another (higher in rank), to pick a quarrel with him Mil.; rgól-bai źu-dón (rgol ba'i zhu don) a speech provoking a quarrel with a superior Mil.; ṗas-rgól (phas rgol) a quarrel or contest begun by the counter-party Sch.; ṗas-rgol-mi (phas rgol mi), ṗas rgól-pa mi (phas rgol pa mi) adversary, opponent Dzl. § 5°, 2. -- sṅá-rgol (snga rgol), and ṗyí-rgol (phyi rgol) (Ssk. पूर्ववादिन् & परवादिन्) 1. acc. to Cs. plaintiff and defendant, but these terms are not quite adequate, as sṅá-rgol (snga rgol) prop. denotes him who begins a quarrel, the aggressor, assailant, both in war and in common life, e.g. in court, and ṗyí-rgol (phyi rgol) designates him, who is intent on defending himself against the attacks and accusations of the opponent, by surpassing him in abusive language and esp. by having recourse to witchcraft. Hence ṗyir-rgól-bai ɣnód-pa (phyir rgol ba'i gnod pa) is a kind of danger against which every one tries to protect himself, and chiefly again by charms and witchcraft. — 2. sṅa-rgol (snga rgol) and ṗyi-rgol (phyi rgol) are also said to signify those students that are contending with one another in academical disputations.
1. resp. ṗyag-rgyá (phyag rgya), seal, stamp, mark, sign, token; (ṗyag-) rgyás _odébs- ((phyag) rgyas 'debs)
